title: Piquant Gooey Japanese Chicken Dorayaki
description: A funky and gooey Japanese dish featuring orange juice and double-fry KFC style chicken, wrapped in a dorayaki form, perfect for midnight cravings. This flexitarian-friendly recipe is a twist on traditional Japanese cuisine.
instructions: In a large bowl, mix together flour, cornstarch, and a pinch of salt.
Add the chicken pieces to the bowl and toss to coat evenly.
Heat 1/4 cup of vegetable oil in a deep frying pan to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
Double-fry the chicken by frying it for 5 minutes, then letting it rest for 5 minutes, and frying it again for an additional 5 minutes.
In a small bowl, whisk together orange juice and soy sauce.
Add the orange juice mixture to the fried chicken and toss to coat.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat and add a small amount of vegetable oil.
Place a dorayaki pancake in the pan and add a few pieces of the chicken mixture to one half of the pancake.
Fold the pancake in half to enclose the filling.
Cook for 2-3 minutes or until the pancake is golden brown and the filling is heated through.
Serve hot and garnish with chopped green onions.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
